metadata.task_description: Redesign ccc-state-controller extension panel UI using modern design system

architecture_patterns: IPC communication between panel and main process

css_structure.pattern: Inline style string within Editor.Panel.extend({ style: `...` })
css_structure.colors: Migrate to CSS variables from hardcoded hex values

ui_components.icons: Monospace text characters, to be replaced with SVG or semantic tokens

user_intent.goal: Redesign ccc-state-controller extension panel UI using ui-ux-pro-max design system with Vibrant & Block-based style
user_intent.scope: Panel styles (style property), HTML template (template property), button system, status indicators, progress components, log display

critical.rationale: PRIMARY TARGET: Contains entire panel UI including inline CSS styles and HTML template. All UI redesign work concentrated here. Current hardcoded hex colors (#333, #f90, #4a4) need migration to CSS variable system.
implementation_focus: Migrate style property from hardcoded values to CSS variables
Replace monospace text icons with SVG or semantic tokens

critical.rationale: PATTERN REFERENCE: Demonstrates modern CSS variable architecture with 50+ design tokens, semantic naming (--primary-color, --success-color), theme support, and component patterns. Template for the redesign.

high.rationale: HTML PATTERN REFERENCE: Shows SVG icon implementation with currentColor fill, semantic class naming, and page-view structure patterns.
implementation_focus: SVG icon patterns for button and status indicators

high.rationale: DESIGN SYSTEM SPEC: Defines accessibility requirements (4.5:1 contrast), icon rules (no emoji, use SVG), transition timing (150-300ms), and common UI rules for professional appearance.
implementation_focus: Follow icon rules (SVG icons, no emoji)

medium.rationale: CONFIG: Defines panel dimensions (420x520, min 380x400). May need adjustment if redesign requires different sizing for new UI components.
INTEGRATION: IPC message handlers for panel operations. UI buttons must continue to trigger correct IPC messages after redesign.

dependency_order: ui-ux-pro-max skill.md (spec) -> CSS variable system design
prioritized_context.sorting_rationale: Critical tier contains the modify target (panel/index.js) and primary pattern reference (styles.css). High tier contains supporting patterns and design system specifications. Medium tier contains configuration and integration points. User intent alignment prioritizes the panel UI file as primary implementation target.

reference_patterns.themes: 6 themes (vibrant-dark, warm-dark, cyberpunk, forest, sunset, ocean)

conflict_detection.mitigation_strategy: Pure CSS/HTML visual redesign within existing panel architecture. No API changes, no IPC changes, no logic changes. Maintain all existing event bindings and element IDs.
constraints_validated: All existing element IDs must be preserved
All existing event listeners must be preserved

summary.key_patterns: CSS variable system, theme support, inline CSS/HTML, native ui-button/ui-input

critical_files.rationale: Primary target file containing entire panel UI with inline CSS styles and HTML template
Reference design system with CSS variables, theming, and comprehensive component patterns

clarification_needs.question: Should the redesigned panel use CSS variables (design tokens) or keep hardcoded values?
clarification_needs.context: The reference package uses a comprehensive CSS variable system with theming support
options: Adopt full CSS variable system with theme support
Use CSS variables for basic tokens only without theme switching
Keep hardcoded values with improved organization

clarification_needs.question: What icon system should be used for buttons and status indicators?
clarification_needs.context: Current panel uses monospace text characters as icons

clarification_needs.question: Should the redesigned panel support multiple themes?
clarification_needs.context: Reference package supports 6 themes via data-theme attribute
options: Single theme only matching Cocos Creator default dark theme
Multiple themes with user preference persistence

constraints.constraint: CSS must be in 'style' property as inline string (cannot reference external CSS file)
HTML must be in 'template' property as inline string
Only Cocos Creator native web components (ui-button, ui-input) are available
No build step allowed - must work directly in Cocos Creator

all_patterns.patterns: CSS variable architecture with semantic naming (--primary-color, --success-color)

typography.heading: Poppins
typography.note: Cocos Creator panels may not support web fonts - use system fallbacks

css_approach.decision: Adopt CSS variable system with basic tokens only
css_approach.rationale: User intent specifies Slate palette and CTA color. Single theme keeps implementation simple while CSS variables provide maintainability. No theme switching complexity.

icon_approach.decision: Semantic text tokens (keep current monospace pattern)
icon_approach.rationale: Cocos Creator 2.x panel constraints. SVG inline would work but adds complexity. Monospace text is consistent with current implementation and meets 'no emoji' constraint.

template_approach.decision: Keep styles and template inline in panel/index.js
template_approach.rationale: No build step allowed. Extraction would require build process. Direct inline editing is simplest approach.
alternatives_considered: Extract to separate files with build step
